01_initial_request.user_intent: To develop a strategy to adaptively steer population evolution based on fitness landscape characteristics, addressing the limitation that no single search strategy is suitable for all fitness landscapes
01_initial_request.quantifiable_goal: Achieve better performance than seven highly performing state-of-the-art DE variants on the benchmark test suite

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Differential Evolution performance depends on fitness landscape characteristics. [Gap] Existing methods do not adaptively steer population evolution based on local fitness landscape. [Decision] To achieve adaptive steering, chose to use Local Fitness Landscape Calculation to perform feature extraction on population, expecting to obtain ruggedness value phi.

parameters.sorting: ascending order of distance from best individual
parameters.optima_counting: c increased by 1 if f(x_km) < f(x_km-1) and f(x_km) < f(x_km+1)

02_agent_trajectory.observation: Local fitness landscape feature value phi calculated, ranging from 0 (unimodal) to 1 (multimodal), and archive cbest obtained.

references.relevance: Introduced approach to count number of optima in landscape to make computations easier.

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Population size is a crucial parameter affecting search efficiency. [Gap] Linear population size reduction does not adjust to different landscape of objective functions. [Decision] To achieve rational resource assignment, chose to use FL-APS Mechanism to perform adaptive population size adjustment, expecting to obtain dynamic N based on phi.
02_agent_trajectory.observation: Population size N adjusted adaptively each generation based on fitness landscape characteristics and function evaluations.

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Mutation strategy guides evolution but single strategy is not suitable for all landscapes. [Gap] DE/current-to-pbest/1 focuses on good fitness making it easy to fall into local optimal. [Decision] To enhance exploration in multimodal landscape, chose to use DE/current-to-pcbest/1 Strategy to perform mutation, expecting to obtain mutant vector using approximate local optimum individuals.

parameters.selection: randomly choose one of top p% individuals from archive cbest

02_agent_trajectory.observation: Mutation strategy defined utilizing individuals of approximate local optimum to increase exploration capability.

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] FL-ADE requires integration of components and parameter adaptation. [Gap] Need to combine FL-APS, mutation strategy, and parameter control. [Decision] To achieve complete algorithm, chose to use FL-ADE Algorithm to perform assembly with LSHADE parameters, expecting to obtain executable procedure.

references.relevance: LSHADE adaptive parameter mechanism and dismiss strategy for infeasible solutions.

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Algorithm performance needs evaluation on standard benchmarks. [Gap] Performance on low dimension problems unknown. [Decision] To achieve validation, chose to use FL-ADE Algorithm to perform optimization on CEC2014 10D benchmarks, expecting to obtain fitness error metrics.
02_agent_trajectory.observation: Mean and standard deviation of fitness error obtained for 10D optimization (Table 3).

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Algorithm performance needs evaluation on standard benchmarks. [Gap] Performance on medium dimension problems unknown. [Decision] To achieve validation, chose to use FL-ADE Algorithm to perform optimization on CEC2014 30D benchmarks, expecting to obtain fitness error metrics.
02_agent_trajectory.observation: Mean and standard deviation of fitness error obtained for 30D optimization (Table 4).

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Algorithm performance needs evaluation on standard benchmarks. [Gap] Performance on high dimension problems unknown. [Decision] To achieve validation, chose to use FL-ADE Algorithm to perform optimization on CEC2014 50D benchmarks, expecting to obtain fitness error metrics.
02_agent_trajectory.observation: Mean and standard deviation of fitness error obtained for 50D optimization (Table 5).

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Performance must be compared against existing methods. [Gap] Relative performance against state-of-the-art unknown. [Decision] To achieve comparison, chose to use 7 State-of-the-Art DE Variants to perform optimization on CEC2014 benchmarks, expecting to obtain baseline fitness error metrics.

parameters.algorithms: EPSDE, MPEDE, CoBiDE, SHADE, LSHADE, LSHADE_cnEpSin, PaDE

02_agent_trajectory.observation: Baseline performance data collected for statistical comparison (Tables 3-5).

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Raw error values need statistical validation. [Gap] Significance of performance difference unknown. [Decision] To achieve rigorous comparison, chose to use Wilcoxon Rank-Sum Test to perform statistical analysis on fitness errors, expecting to obtain significance symbols (+, -, =).
02_agent_trajectory.observation: FL-ADE performs better than other seven highly performing state-of-art DE variants on most benchmarks.

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Effectiveness of FL-APS needs verification. [Gap] Contribution of FL-APS vs linear reduction unknown. [Decision] To achieve component validation, chose to use FL-ADE_linear to perform optimization with Linear Population Size Reduction, expecting to obtain comparative performance.
02_agent_trajectory.observation: FL-ADE with FL-APS obtained 26/26/23 better or similar performances compared to FL-ADE_linear on 10D/30D/50D.

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Effectiveness of mutation strategy needs verification. [Gap] Contribution of DE/current-to-pcbest vs others unknown. [Decision] To achieve component validation, chose to use DE with Different Mutation Strategies to perform optimization, expecting to obtain comparative ranking.
02_agent_trajectory.observation: DE/current-to-pcbest/1 gets the first ranking; other strategies performed worse.

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Parameter sensitivity impacts performance. [Gap] Optimal values for N_init and FES_t unknown. [Decision] To achieve parameter tuning, chose to use Friedman Test to perform sensitivity analysis on N_init and FES_t, expecting to obtain optimal settings.
02_agent_trajectory.observation: N=25 log(D) sqrt(D) and FES_t=(1/2)*FES_max achieve better average ranking; other values suboptimal.

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Parameter pc determines strategy performance. [Gap] Optimal value for pc unknown. [Decision] To achieve parameter tuning, chose to use Friedman Test to perform sensitivity analysis on pc, expecting to obtain optimal setting.
02_agent_trajectory.observation: pc=0.3 saw the best average ranking; other values suboptimal.

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Algorithm efficiency is critical for practical use. [Gap] Computational complexity of FL-ADE unknown. [Decision] To achieve efficiency validation, chose to use Runtime Complexity Measurement to perform time analysis, expecting to obtain complexity class.

parameters.environment: MATLAB R2018b, Intel Core i7-9700, 8 GB RAM, Windows 10

02_agent_trajectory.observation: FL-ADE inherits the linear complexity of classical DE.

references.relevance: Reference for computing time measurement protocol.

03_success_verification.validation_technique: Benchmark Testing on CEC2014 with Wilcoxon Rank-Sum Test

fitness_error_mean_std.interpretation: FL-ADE achieves better or similar results on 26/26/26 (10D), 26/28/26 (30D), 26/29/24 (50D) benchmarks compared to competitors.

wilcoxon_test_results.interpretation: FL-ADE is significantly better than seven highly performing state-of-art DE variants on most benchmarks.

ablation_study_results.interpretation: FL-APS obtained 26/26/23 better or similar performances compared to linear reduction on 10D/30D/50D.

03_success_verification.final_verdict: Objective achieved: FL-ADE algorithm performs better than the other seven highly performing state-of-art DE variants on the CEC2014 benchmark test suite.
